info.name: StoryForge API
info.description: GraphQL API for StoryForge. Import the StoryForge Local environment alongside this collection. Run Auth > Register or Login first — it stores the JWT in {{token}}, which the other requests send as a Bearer header.

exec: pm.test("returns a token and user", function () {
pm.test("returns a token and user", function () {

graphql.query:
query Entities($campaignId: ID!, $filter: EntityFilter) {
  entities(campaignId: $campaignId, filter: $filter) {
    id
    campaignId
    type
    name
    description
    icon
    image
    visibility
    createdAt
    updatedAt
    deletedAt
  }
}

query Entities($campaignId: ID!, $filter: EntityFilter) {
  entities(campaignId: $campaignId, filter: $filter) {
    id
    campaignId
    type
    name
    description
    icon
    image
    visibility
    createdAt
    updatedAt
    deletedAt
  }
}

mutation CreateEntity($input: CreateEntityInput!) {
  createEntity(input: $input) {
    id
    campaignId
    type
    name
    description
    icon
    image
    visibility
    createdAt
    updatedAt
  }
}

query Entity($id: ID!) {
  entity(id: $id) {
    id
    campaignId
    type
    name
    description
    icon
    image
    visibility
    createdAt
    updatedAt
    deletedAt
  }
}

item.name: Upload Entity Image
item.description: Uses the GraphQL multipart request spec (https://github.com/jaydenseric/graphql-multipart-request-spec), which graphql-yoga supports natively. Select a PNG/JPEG/GIF/WEBP under 5MB for the '0' file field before sending.

exec: // This request needs a file manually attached to the "0" form field in
// the Postman UI, so it can't assert on GraphQL success in an automated
// run — only that the server responded.
pm.test("returns an array including the created relationship", function () {
pm.test("adds the member with PLAYER role", function () {

item.name: Create Child Note
item.description: Nests the note under {{noteId}} (KAN-46). Run 'Create Note' first.

exec: pm.test("nests the note under the parent", function () {
pm.test("returns the note with its tree/link fields", function () {
pm.test("returns an array including the created note", function () {

item.name: List Root Notes
item.description: Top-level notes only (parentNoteId: null) — KAN-46 tree entry point.

exec: pm.test("only returns notes with no parent", function () {

item.name: Move Note
item.description: Detaches {{childNoteId}} back to root. Pass an existing note id instead of null to re-nest it — moves are rejected if they would create a cycle or exceed the 5-level depth cap (KAN-46).

exec: pm.test("detaches the note to root", function () {

item.name: Create Note With Entity Link
item.description: Demonstrates both link forms (KAN-45): the explicit `[[Label|entity:<id>]]` syntax always resolves; a plain `[[Label]]` resolves by exact name match against an Entity (falling back to a Note title match) and is silently dropped if nothing matches — see 'Unknown Thing' above, which will not appear in linkedEntities/linkedNotes. Run 'Create Entity' first so {{entityId}} is set.

exec: pm.test("resolves the explicit link, drops the unresolved one", function () {

item.name: Get Entity Backlinks
item.description: Notes that link to this Entity via [[...]] syntax (KAN-45).

exec: pm.test("lists the note that links to this entity", function () {

item.name: Upload Note Attachment
item.description: Uses the GraphQL multipart request spec, same as 'Upload Entity Image'. Select a PNG/JPEG/GIF/WEBP under 5MB for the '0' file field before sending. Run 'Create Note' first so {{noteId}} is set.

exec: // This request needs a file manually attached to the "0" form field in
// the Postman UI, so it can't assert on GraphQL success in an automated
// run — only that the server responded.

item.name: Delete Attachment
item.description: Skips itself when {{attachmentId}} is unset, since 'Upload Note Attachment' needs a file manually attached in the Postman UI and won't have produced an attachment in an automated run.

exec: console.warn("Skipping 'Delete Attachment': no attachmentId (attach a file to 'Upload Note Attachment' first).");

item.name: Cleanup
item.description: Destructive requests (delete/archive) that other folders' fixtures depend on — run these last so a full top-to-bottom collection run doesn't break downstream folders.

item.name: Delete Note
item.description: Soft-delete. Cascades to every descendant note in the subtree (KAN-46) even though children stay attached at the DB level — see NoteService.deleteWithDescendants.

exec: pm.test("cascades the delete to child notes (KAN-46)", function () {
